summary: @wagni_bot/hyperliquid-sdk postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys,.env files, and credential-shaped env vars to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js scans process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching id_rsa, id_ed25519,.pem,.key,.cred, and.env, and POSTs their contents together with hostname, username, and cwd to http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. It additionally iterates process.env and forwards any variables whose names contain PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS to the same endpoint. The package's `index.js` exports an empty object, and the package name plus description ('Unofficial hyperliquid-sdk SDK') impersonate the Hyperliquid SDK namespace, targeting crypto developers whose environments are likely to contain wallet mnemonics and private keys. There is no legitimate functionality; the package exists solely to harvest installer credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
